entity.country_or_origin: Hong Kong / British Virgin Islands / global
entity.summary: Major centralized cryptocurrency exchange founded in 2012 and operated by iFinex. HEI records Bitfinex as an active exchange with event-backed history, including a 2016 CFTC order, the 2016 Bitfinex hack, and the 2022 U.S. seizure of bitcoin connected to the hack.
entity.notes: Added as a fresh-discovery event-backed record rather than from the verified-unadded 1000-row backlog. Bitfinex remains active, so this is not a shutdown record. The 2022 DOJ case concerns laundering and recovery of stolen funds connected to the 2016 hack; it is not recorded as a finding that Bitfinex itself committed wrongdoing.

events.title: CFTC ordered Bitfinex to pay civil monetary penalty
events.description: The U.S. CFTC ordered Bitfinex to pay a $75,000 civil monetary penalty over financed retail commodity transactions and registration-related findings.
events.notes: Civil regulatory order; it did not make Bitfinex dead.

events.title: Bitfinex suffered major bitcoin theft
events.description: Bitfinex suffered a major security breach in August 2016. Public reporting and DOJ materials describe roughly 119,754 bitcoin stolen from the exchange.
events.notes: The exchange later continued operating; this event records the breach, not a shutdown.

events.title: U.S. authorities seized bitcoin connected to the 2016 Bitfinex hack
events.description: The U.S. Department of Justice announced arrests and the seizure of more than $3.6 billion in bitcoin connected to the 2016 Bitfinex hack, calling it the department's largest financial seizure at the time.
events.notes: This is a recovery/law-enforcement event related to the stolen funds. It does not imply Bitfinex was the accused party in the laundering case.

events.title: Bitfinex verified as active exchange entity
events.description: Bitfinex was verified as an active centralized exchange through its official website and public reference material.
events.notes: Fresh-discovery record; no verified-unadded backlog row was consumed.

evidence.notes: Temporary secondary reference for CFTC chronology. Replace with full order PDF if later added.

evidence.title: US married couple arrested for allegedly conspiring to launder $4.5bn in bitcoin
evidence.notes: Report summarizing the 2016 hack and 119,754 bitcoin amount in the 2022 law-enforcement context.

evidence.title: DOJ says it seized $3.6 billion in stolen bitcoin from 2016 hack
evidence.notes: Report on the 2016 hack amount and 2022 DOJ seizure/arrests.

evidence.title: When stolen crypto is recovered, who is entitled to restitution?
evidence.notes: Reuters legal analysis summarizing the 2016 Bitfinex hack, later recovery, and restitution context.

evidence.notes: DOJ announcement for the 2022 arrests and seizure connected to the 2016 Bitfinex hack.

evidence.title: DOJ says it seized $3.6 billion in stolen bitcoin from 2016 hack
evidence.notes: News report on DOJ's 2022 seizure and arrests.

evidence.notes: Contextual reporting on the federal recovery investigation.
